task_completion.description: Configure the Liver segment color to tan/brown.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the segment list or 3D view, the segment named 'liver' appears tan/brown (approximately RGB 200, 150, 100).

task_completion.description: Configure the Liver segment 3D opacity to 40%.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D view, the 'liver' segment is semi-transparent, allowing internal structures to be seen through it, or the opacity slider for the liver is visibly set to approximately 40% in the GUI.

task_completion.description: Configure the Tumor segment color to bright red.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the segment list or 3D view, the segment named 'tumour' appears bright red (approximately RGB 255, 0, 0).

task_completion.description: Configure the Tumor segment 3D opacity to 100%.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D view, the 'tumour' segment appears solid and fully opaque, or its opacity slider is visibly set to 100% in the GUI.

task_completion.description: Configure the Portal Vein segment color to blue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the segment list or 3D view, the segment named 'venaporta' appears blue (approximately RGB 0, 0, 255).

task_completion.description: Configure the Portal Vein segment 3D opacity to 100%.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D view, the 'venaporta' segment appears solid and fully opaque, or its opacity slider is visibly set to 100% in the GUI.

task_completion.description: Ensure all three segments (Liver, Tumor, Portal Vein) are visible in the 3D view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view simultaneously displays the semi-transparent tan liver, the solid red tumor(s), and the solid blue portal vein. The 'eye' (visibility) icons next to all three segments in the segment list should be toggled on.

integrity.description: The agent used the medical imaging software's GUI to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the software's UI panels (e.g., Segmentations module, color pickers, opacity sliders) rather than terminal commands or a text editor.

integrity.description: The 3D view shows a genuine rendering of the anatomical segments.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer contains the expected complex anatomical shapes (liver, branching vessels, tumors) that respond to the display property changes, not an imported static image.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply paste the task description into a text file to fake completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual output is strictly within the medical imaging application's rendering window and segment property lists.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset uses specific naming conventions for the segments: the Liver is named 'liver', the Portal Vein is named 'venaporta', and the Tumor is named 'tumour'. You must look for these specific names in the segment list when verifying the color and opacity configurations.
